articles.title: LPG consumption declined 19% in May, petrol-diesel increased
articles.description: India's LPG consumption fell 19% in May, while petrol and diesel usage saw slight increases amid ongoing supply disruptions.

articles.title: FOREX-Dollar in tight range as traders eye Middle East peace talks
articles.description: A peace deal between the U.S. and Iran would ease pressure on currencies of oil-importing countries such as Japan and euro zone nations, while reducing safe-haven demand ‌for the dollar. U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that talks with Iran were ongoing, a comment that pushed oil prices lower despite a report that Tehran had suspended indirect negotiations with Washington to end hostilities.

articles.title: The worst summer job market in 80 years has teens scrambling for swimming pool and ice cream jobs
articles.description: High schoolers haven’t faced this tough of a summer job market since 1948. Rising inflation, higher oil prices, and a slow hiring market are to blame.

articles.title: Teens are up against the worst summer job market in nearly 80 years-they’re fighting against hundreds to work at ice cream shops and swimming pools
articles.description: High schoolers haven’t faced this tough of a summer job market since 1948. Rising inflation, higher oil prices, and a slow hiring market are to blame.

articles.title: LDF stages walkout in Kerala Assembly, accusing UDF of neglecting fuel crisis
articles.description: The Opposition Left Democratic Front (LDF) staged a walkout in the Kerala Legislative Assembly on Tuesday after accusing the United Democratic Front (UDF) government of dismissing the plight of ordinary people, who have been hit the hardest by the surge in fuel and cooking gas prices.

articles.title: Iran talks ‘very boring.’ Trump says he doesn’t care if they’re over
articles.description: Trump also said he was not worried about oil prices.

articles.title: Wall Street's record-breaking rally slows as Alphabet drags on the market
articles.description: Wall Street's record-breaking rally is slowing following a drop for Alphabet, one of Wall Street’s most influential stocks. The S&P 500 slipped 0.1% Tuesday, a day after setting its latest all-time high. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 65 points, and the Nasdaq composite fell 0.3%. Analysts have said the market may be set for a slowdown following an unrelenting streak of nine straight winning weeks for the S&P 500. Alphabet fell after saying it plans to raise $80 billion through sales of stock to pay for its AI investments. AI chip companies rose. Oil prices held steadier following Monday's bounce back.

articles.title: US stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 edge lower as Middle East tensions keep markets on edge
articles.description: US stock futures showed a muted start as tensions in the Middle East affected investor sentiment. S&P 500 futures dipped 0.1%, while Nasdaq futures fell 0.1%. Despite the market's recent record highs, concerns over rising oil prices and tech stock performance lingered.
